Table of contents

  • Setup
  • Load and merge the two datasets
  • Standard workflow on the merged (uncorrected) data
  • Integrate: MNN, Harmony, CCA
  • Compare integrations
  • Label transfer: annotate pbmc4k from pbmc3k

Integration: horizontal integration of PBMC3k + PBMC4k, and label transfer

Published

July 21, 2026

Setup

setRepositories(ind = 1:3)
required.packages <- c("Seurat", "BiocManager", "harmony", "glmGamPoi", "remotes", "batchelor")
InstallMissing <- function(packages) {
    for (pkg in packages) {
        if (!require(pkg, character.only = TRUE)) {
            install.packages(pkg, dependencies = TRUE)
            library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
        }
    }
}

remotes::install_github("satijalab/seurat-wrappers")
Using GitHub PAT from the git credential store.
Skipping install of 'SeuratWrappers' from a github remote, the SHA1 (8df83435) has not changed since last install.
  Use `force = TRUE` to force installation
library(Seurat)
Loading required package: SeuratObject
Loading required package: sp
'SeuratObject' was built under R 4.6.0 but the current version is
4.6.1; it is recomended that you reinstall 'SeuratObject' as the ABI
for R may have changed

Attaching package: 'SeuratObject'

    intersect, t
library(dplyr)

Attaching package: 'dplyr'
The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag
The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(SeuratWrappers)

Load and merge the two datasets

Download the query dataset pbmc4k which we will annotate from here and the annotated pbmc3k dataset from here. Note that in an ideal world, your reference annotated object will have more cells than the query.

# EDIT THIS
PBMC3k_path <- "~/github/SSGIM2026/single_cell/pbmc3k_annotated.rds"
PBMC4k_path <- "~/github/SSGIM2026/single_cell/pbmc4k.rds"

pbmc3k <- readRDS(PBMC3k_path)
pbmc3k$dataset <- "pbmc3k"
pbmc4k <- readRDS(PBMC4k_path)
pbmc4k$dataset <- "pbmc4k"

# Merge the two datasets
merged <- merge(pbmc3k, y = pbmc4k, add.cell.ids = c("pbmc3k", "pbmc4k"))

Standard workflow on the merged (uncorrected) data

merged <- NormalizeData(merged) %>%
  FindVariableFeatures(selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 2000) %>%
  ScaleData() %>%
  RunPCA(npcs = 30) %>%
  FindNeighbors(dims = 1:20) %>%
  RunUMAP(dims = 1:20)

Normalizing layer: counts.2

# Do the datasets merge into each other well?
DimPlot(merged, reduction = "umap", group.by = "dataset")
merged <- FindClusters(merged, resolution = 0.3)

merged$merged_cluster <- Idents(merged)

DimPlot(merged, reduction = "umap", group.by = "merged_cluster", label = TRUE)
DimPlot(merged, reduction = "umap", group.by = c("dataset", "merged_cluster"), label = TRUE)

Integrate: MNN, Harmony, CCA

merged <- JoinLayers(merged)
merged[["RNA"]] <- split(merged[["RNA"]], f = merged$dataset)
Splitting 'counts', 'data' layers. Not splitting 'scale.data.1', 'scale.data'. If you would like to split other layers, set in `layers` argument.
merged <- NormalizeData(merged) %>% FindVariableFeatures() %>%
  ScaleData() %>%
  RunPCA()

integrated <-  IntegrateLayers(object = merged, method = FastMNNIntegration,
                                   orig.reduction = "pca",
                                   new.reduction = "integrated.mnn",
                                          verbose = FALSE)
Warning in .local(x, ...): 'normalizeCounts' is deprecated.

Use 'scrapper::normalizeCounts' instead.
See help("Deprecated")
Warning: Layer counts isn't present in the assay object; returning NULL
integrated <- IntegrateLayers(
    object = integrated, method = HarmonyIntegration,
    orig.reduction = "pca", new.reduction = "integrated.harmony",
    verbose = FALSE
)
The `features` argument is ignored by `HarmonyIntegration`.
This message is displayed once per session.
integrated <- IntegrateLayers(
    object = integrated, method = CCAIntegration,
    orig.reduction = "pca", new.reduction = "integrated.cca",
    verbose = FALSE
)

Label transfer: annotate pbmc4k from pbmc3k

We can use the same idea to transfer annotation from pbmc3k to pbmc4k.

pbmc3k <- NormalizeData(pbmc3k) %>%
    FindVariableFeatures(selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 2000) %>%
    ScaleData() %>%
    RunPCA(npcs = 30) %>%
    FindNeighbors(dims = 1:20) %>%
    RunUMAP(dims = 1:20)

pbmc4k <- NormalizeData(pbmc4k) %>%
    FindVariableFeatures(selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 2000) %>%
    ScaleData()  %>%
    RunPCA(npcs = 30) %>%
    FindNeighbors(dims = 1:20) %>%
    RunUMAP(dims = 1:20)

anchors <- FindTransferAnchors(reference = pbmc3k,
                               query = pbmc4k,
                               dims = 1:30,
                               reference.reduction = "pca")
Projecting cell embeddings
Finding neighborhoods
Finding anchors
    Found 4767 anchors
predictions <- TransferData(anchorset = anchors, refdata = pbmc3k$seurat_annotations,
                            dims = 1:30)
Finding integration vectors
Finding integration vector weights
Predicting cell labels
pbmc4k <- AddMetaData(pbmc4k, metadata = predictions)
DimPlot(pbmc4k, group.by = "predicted.id")

UMAP is only for visualisation – always check using markers if the results make sense.

markers.B <- FindMarkers(object = pbmc4k, ident.1 = "B", only.pos = TRUE, group.by = "predicted.id")
For a (much!) faster implementation of the Wilcoxon Rank Sum Test,
(default method for FindMarkers) please install the presto package
--------------------------------------------
install.packages('devtools')
devtools::install_github('immunogenomics/presto')
--------------------------------------------
After installation of presto, Seurat will automatically use the more 
efficient implementation (no further action necessary).
